channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Oil Prices Hold Steady as Iran Talks Remain Murky
item.description: Investors waited for news on the situation in the Middle East, as the U.S. and Iran gave mixed signals on peace negotiations.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amr Alfiky/Reuters

item.title: War and Energy Shortages Boost China’s Influence in Asia
item.description: The war in Iran has left China’s neighbors appealing for help, handing Beijing the kind of sway it has long sought.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Joyce Zhou/Reuters
item.media:description: Oil storage tanks at a depot in Hong Kong. Shipments of Chinese oil products to select countries have offered relief during the war and underscored how Beijing engenders good will in times of crisis, experts say.

item.title: The ‘Perfect Storm’ Hanging Over Britain’s Public Debt
item.description: Bond investors are expected to scrutinize the results of critical local elections on Thursday as the war in the Middle East adds inflation pressure on the government.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:credit: Joseph Horton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Investors are betting the Bank of England will approve several interest rate increases this year, reversing previous expectations that rates would come down.

item.title: Anthropic’s C.E.O. Says It Could Grow by 80 Times This Year
item.description: The chief executive, Dario Amodei, said the rapid growth had exponentially increased the start-up’s need for more computing power.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha
item.media:credit: Denis Balibouse/Reuters
item.media:description: Anthropic’s chief executive, Dario Amodei, in January. On Wednesday, he spoke at his company’s annual developer conference.

item.title: JPMorgan Tried to Settle Harassment Accusations Against Executive
item.description: The bank offered $1 million before a former employee filed a lawsuit against a woman who was an executive director on his team, two people briefed on the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland
item.media:credit: Kylie Cooper/Reuters
item.media:description: A New York lawsuit accuses JPMorgan Chase of allowing one of its investment bankers to make sexual advances and racially motivated comments to a lower-ranking employee.

item.title: GameStop’s CEO Ryan Cohen Baffles Fans With eBay Bid
item.description: Investors questioned Ryan Cohen’s latest gambit after he gave an evasive CNBC interview and a once-loyal supporter sold his GameStop shares.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch

item.title: Attack on French Cargo Ship Highlights Continued Risks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. Central Command and the ship’s owner disagreed on whether the vessel coordinated safe passage with the United States military.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman on Wednesday.

item.title: Rupert Murdoch, David Zaslav and More Media Moguls Remember Ted Turner, CNN’s Founder
item.description: The CNN founder’s death was met with encomiums from leaders in the media industry, which he helped reinvent. “He will be remembered for generations,” said one.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Brian F Alpert/ZUMA Press Wire, via Shutterstock
item.media:description: Ted Turner in the 1980s.

item.title: Higher Gas Prices Are Hitting Lower-Income Americans the Hardest
item.description: People with lower incomes are driving less and devoting more of their budgets to transport as gas prices soar.
item.dc:creator: Talmon Joseph Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Federal Reserve Bank of New York
item.media:credit: Jenny Kane/Associated Press
item.media:description: Gas prices have soared since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Disney Posts Strong Earnings Despite Slowdown in Park Visitors
item.description: Disney parks are closely watched as a bellwether for consumer confidence.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Disney’s former chief executive Bob Iger and current chief executive, Josh D’Amaro, at Disneyland’s 70th anniversary commemoration last year.

item.title: Ted Turner, Creator of CNN and the 24-Hour News Cycle, Dies at 87
item.description: As one of the most important figures in media history, he oversaw a vast cable empire of news, sports and entertainment channels.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Kandell
item.media:credit: Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images
item.media:description: Ted Turner was a visionary media mogul who dominated the cable television industry, and extended his reach into movies, major league baseball and even yachting.

item.title: Why Is Gas So Much More Expensive in Some States Than Others?
item.description: Shipping, taxes, competition and environmental regulations all play a part in the cost of gasoline. That can lead to stark differences in pump prices.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: The national average gas price has soared about 50 percent since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Inside the Debate About Adding Stocks to Trump Accounts
item.description: Trump administration officials are weighing a plan that would allow wealthy individuals to donate shares in their companies to the investment accounts.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Brian O’Keefe
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: Trump administration officials have discussed a major expansion of so-called Trump accounts.

item.title: Justin Wolfers, Cable’s Favorite Economist, Joins the Creator Economy
item.description: With his crisp breakdowns of President Trump’s trade moves and distinctive Aussie accent, Mr. Wolfers has become a TV constant. Now he’s starting his own media company.
item.dc:creator: Brian O’Keefe
item.media:credit: Rosem Morton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Justin Wolfers at his home in Ann Arbor, Mich.

item.title: The New York Times Passes 13 Million Subscribers
item.description: The company’s adjusted operating profit hit $117.9 million in the quarter, up 27.2 percent from a year earlier, on revenue of $712.2 million.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The New York Times Company has set a goal of 15 million subscribers by the end of next year.

item.title: American Factories Lag in Adopting A.I. This Drugmaker Is an Exception.
item.description: A Bristol Myers Squibb plant that makes cancer drugs was the only manufacturer in the U.S. recognized for innovation by the World Economic Forum this year.
item.dc:creator: Farah Stockman
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: At the Bristol Myers Squibb facility in Devens, Mass., scientists use automated machines to help make cancer drugs.

item.title: Oil Prices Fall on Hope U.S.-Iran War Turmoil in the Gulf May Be Easing
item.description: Oil prices fell and stocks rose after President Trump’s about-face to pause the U.S. operation to escort commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt, Emmett Lindner and Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Brandon Bell/Getty Images

item.title: Guy Goma’s Accidental BBC Interview Lives On After 20 Years
item.description: Guy Goma thought he was interviewing for an I.T. job when he found himself on air on the BBC. What came next was familiar to anyone who has been unprepared at work.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe

item.title: ‘Avatar’ Suit Focuses on Hot Topic in A.I. Age: A Character’s Face
item.description: An actress accused the director James Cameron of stealing her likeness for a digitally created, blue-skinned warrior princess.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Tell Us How High Gas Prices Have Affected Your Finances
item.description: With fuel costs soaring from the war with Iran, we want to hear how you’re feeling about the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: Gas prices have soared since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Modi’s Triumph in West Bengal Elections Puts Him Closer to an Opposition-Free India
item.description: With his triumph in West Bengal state elections, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has moved closer to his dream of an opposition-free India.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli, Hari Kumar and Pragati K.B.
item.media:credit: Sahiba Chawdhary/Reuters
item.media:description: Supporters of Mr. Modi’s Bharatiya Janata Party celebrating in Kolkata as the party headed to victory in the West Bengal state assembly elections on Monday.

item.title: U.S. Sues The New York Times, Claiming Discrimination Against a White Man
item.description: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission said the paper had engaged in “unlawful employment practices” against the man, who did not get a sought-after promotion.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Davis O’Brien
item.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission has been investigating a discrimination claim filed last year against The New York Times by a white male employee.

item.title: Delta Air Lines Will Stop Serving Snacks and Drinks on Short Flights
item.description: Most travelers flying less than 350 miles on Delta will soon have to go without free coffee and cookies, but the carrier is adding service to longer flights.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:credit: Stephen Maturen/Getty Images
item.media:description: About 9 percent of Delta’s daily flights will no longer have free snack and beverage service after May 19.

item.title: Coinbase Lays Off 14% of Employees as A.I. Changes Work
item.description: The largest U.S. crypto exchange said it was cutting jobs because of cryptocurrency market volatility and to “optimize” for the artificial intelligence era.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha
item.media:credit: Gabby Jones for The New York Times
item.media:description: Coinbase, which went public in 2021, is cutting 14 percent of its work force.

item.title: James Murdoch’s Company Said to Be in Talks to Acquire Major Parts of Vox Media
item.description: A deal for Vox Media, which generates more than $80 million through podcasts alone, would elevate Mr. Murdoch’s company, Lupa Systems, as a major player in U.S. media.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Jessica Testa
item.media:credit: Jared Soares for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Murdoch, the younger son of the media mogul Rupert Murdoch, in Washington, D.C., in 2020.

item.title: The FBI Searched a Washington Post Reporter’s Home. She Is Now a Pulitzer Prize Winner.
item.description: Hannah Natanson’s reporting anchored a package of articles from The Washington Post that won a Pulitzer on Monday, four months after agents seized her devices.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner, via, via Reuters
item.media:description: Hannah Natanson, a reporter and member of the winning team for the Pulitzer Prize for Public Service, in the Washington Post newsroom on Monday.

item.title: Maersk Ship Passed Strait of Hormuz Under U.S. Military Protection
item.description: A U.S.-flagged ship operated by a Maersk subsidiary exited under American military guidance, part of President Trump’s effort to encourage ships to pass the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fatima Shbair/Associated Press
item.media:description: Ships in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz on Friday.

item.title: Inside the Met Gala After-Parties: See the Stars and Their Looks
item.description: Models, designers, D.J.s and performers kept the night going into the early morning.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein and Sarah Bahr
item.media:credit: Rebecca Smeyne for The New York Times

item.title: The Federal Safety Net Isn’t Ready for Artificial Intelligence
item.description: As fears of A.I.-driven job losses mount, economists warn that unemployment benefits and other programs to help displaced workers aren’t sufficient.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman and Tony Romm
item.media:credit: Felix Decombat

item.title: Ford Says an Affordable Electric Pickup Truck is Still Coming Next Year
item.description: Ford Motor has written off $20 billion in electric vehicle investments but says it is forging ahead with an electric pickup that will sell for $30,000 next year.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: Sports Utility Vehicles and Light Trucks
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kate Medley for The New York Times
item.media:description: Recharging a Mustang Mach-E, Ford’s only electric vehicle right now.

item.title: U.S. Trade Deficit Grew in March
item.description: Both exports and imports rose after the Supreme Court struck down many of the president’s highest levies in February.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Ruth Fremson/The New York Times
item.media:description: The data provided the first snapshot of trade since a ruling by the Supreme Court forced major changes to the Trump administration’s tariffs.

item.title: Behind the Trump Administration’s Potential Rethink on A.I.
item.description: Artificial intelligence has become a national security concern. That has federal officials rethinking how lightly it should regulate the technology.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Lauren Hirsch and Vivienne Walt
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: Is the White House moving away from the laissez-faire approach to artificial intelligence championed by David Sacks, its former A.I. czar?

item.title: Exploring Dublin on a Budget
item.description: Here are five ways to explore the energetic Irish capital without spending a fortune. Free music and storytelling play a role.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Glusac and Michael Vince Kim
item.media:description: A horse-drawn carriage takes tourists past St. Patrick’s Tower in the Liberties neighborhood in Dublin.

item.title: Secret Recordings, Hidden Shares and a Family Rift at the South Korean Giant LG
item.description: An inheritance dispute over the former chairman’s estate has prompted a criminal complaint over how the dynastic chaebol family behind LG divides its assets.
item.dc:creator: Daisuke Wakabayashi and Grace Moon
item.media:credit: Illustration by Max-o-matic. Photographs by Getty Images, Associated Press, Chang W. Lee/The New York Times, Shutterstock.

item.title: Oil Prices Slide but Remain Elevated as U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Is Tested
item.description: Oil prices fell, giving back some of the gains from the previous day as attacks in the Middle East raised concerns about the war starting again.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Stephane Mahe/Reuters

item.title: China’s Big Bet on Wind Power Is Paying Off
item.description: An industrial policy of subsidies and import restrictions laid the foundations for China to become almost as dominant in wind turbines as in solar panels.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher and Qilai Shen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Amazon Expands Logistics Arm to Outside Companies
item.description: The company said its shipping, fulfillment and delivery services would be offered to other businesses. Several large corporations have already signed on.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.media:credit: Octavio Jones for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Amazon fulfillment center in St. Petersburg, Fla. Amazon has a fleet of more than 80,000 trailers and 100 aircraft that operate through company partnerships.

item.title: New York Times Wins 3 Pulitzer Prizes
item.description: The Washington Post won the award for public service, considered the most prestigious of the Pulitzers, for its coverage of the Trump administration’s overhaul of federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: A photo that was part of the Pulitzer Prize-winning entry by Saher Alghorra, a contributor to The New York Times, in the breaking news photo category. It showed a child wounded in Gaza City being transferred to a hospital in April last year.

item.title: Pulitzer Prizes: 2026 Winners List
item.description: Here is the full list of winners and finalists.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: This image, showing displaced Palestinians walking to Gaza City in October 2025, was part of the entry by Saher Alghorra, a contributor to The New York Times who won for breaking news photography.

item.title: Anthropic and Wall Street Giants Join Forces to Create New A.I. Firm
item.description: Blackstone and Goldman Sachs are among the investors in the new firm, which will help integrate Anthropic’s A.I. model Claude into their systems.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Farrell
item.media:credit: Marissa Leshnov for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Anthropic office in San Francisco.

item.title: Doris Fisher, Co-Founder of the Gap, Dies at 94
item.description: The company she started in 1969 with her husband, Don, grew from a single store selling jeans and records to a $16 billion brand that remade the apparel industry.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Rifkin
item.media:credit: Pool photo/Hector Amezcua, via Reuters
item.media:description: Doris Fisher in 2011, when she was inducted into the California Hall of Fame along with her late husband, Don.

item.title: U.S. Warns China Over Iranian Oil as Sanctions Fight Intensifies
item.description: China told its independent refineries to disregard U.S. sanctions over their purchases of Iranian crude.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The warning came from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who is expected to participate in high-stakes meetings this month between the United States and China.

item.title: The Return for These Investors Isn’t Money, It’s More Affordable Housing
item.description: Local governments are trying to create housing that is permanently affordable by investing directly in construction. They are rewriting how housing programs have traditionally operated.
item.dc:creator: Conor Dougherty
item.media:credit: Julie Holder for The New York Times
item.media:description: Matt Bedsole of Invest Chattanooga has helped seed housing funds in several cities.

item.title: Europeans Prepare for a More Dangerous World in a Time of Economic Upheaval
item.description: A stalwart on defense spending, Poland is training its citizens in civil defense while balancing their jobs and families.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.media:credit: Maciek Nabrdalik for The New York Times
item.media:description: Polish civilians gathered at the headquarters of the 13th Silesian Territorial Defense Brigade in southern Poland for emergency preparedness training.

item.title: Confusion in Strait of Hormuz Leaves Shipping Firms Guessing
item.description: The U.S. vowed to help tankers navigate the perilous conditions that have kept them stranded in the Persian Gulf, but it remained unclear if companies would try to get out.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Gregory Schmidt and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Ships and boats in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman on Monday.

item.title: What Was Discussed at Google’s White House Meeting About A.I.
item.description: The technology giant met with administration officials last week to address a growing concern in Washington: insufficient computing power for artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Brian O’Keefe
item.media:credit: Jeenah Moon for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sundar Pichai, Alphabet’s C.E.O., pictured here at the 2024 DealBook Summit, was in Washington last week to discuss new worries about A.I.

item.title: GameStop Proposes Acquiring eBay for $55 Billion
item.description: Analysts question the feasibility of this long-shot bid by the video game retailer to acquire the much larger online marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch
item.media:credit: Chona Kasinger for The New York Times
item.media:description: GameStop has proposed that Ryan Cohen would serve as chief executive of a combined company.

item.title: A 22,000-Home Community Is Being Built in a 5,000-Person Town
item.description: Chatham Park, a master-planned community in Pittsboro, N.C., is expected to expand the town to 60,000 people when completed.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Abrams
item.media:credit: Sebastian Siadecki for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Chatham County Historic Courthouse in downtown Pittsboro, N.C.

item.title: ‘City of Glass’ Is Under Threat From Iran War
item.description: In India, Firozabad has depended on its glassmaking industry for centuries. Fuel prices are starting to shatter businesses already under pressure.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli, Suhasini Raj and Atul Loke
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Chizi, Standup Comic Exiled in China, Wants to Be More Than Just ‘a Rebel Comedian’
item.description: Having gotten into trouble for making jokes critical of the Chinese government, the standup comic Chizi now lives in self-imposed exile. He’s finding that freedom imposes its own constraints.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan and Chang W. Lee
item.media:description: Chizi performed for the first time in years in Tokyo in April. The audience went to see a rebel.
